decision: Initialize decision log
reason: Create persistent decision memory for agent workflow
impact: Improves traceability

decision: Adopt feature-oriented backend packages under main/features
reason: Prevent monolithic file growth and align code layout with PMO functional domains
impact: Improves maintainability, team parallelism, and roadmap scalability

decision: Keep legacy API endpoints while introducing /api/pmo/* scaffold routes
reason: Avoid frontend breakage during incremental migration
impact: Safe transition path with backward compatibility

decision: Mirror backend feature domains in frontend under features/pmo/modules/*
reason: Keep frontend and backend architecture isomorphic for maintainability and team scaling
impact: Reduces coupling and avoids oversized UI/API files

decision: Introduce authGet/authPost in api store as shared transport contract
reason: Standardize authenticated requests across all PMO feature modules
impact: Consistent API integration pattern and lower duplication

decision: Track roadmap as machine-readable task graph in repository
reason: Need deterministic source for auto-creating Bitrix24 tasks and dependency links
impact: Enables repeatable roadmap publication and status sync across chats

decision: Implement Bitrix24 sync CLI with dry-run default
reason: Prevent accidental writes and allow validation before applying to production project board
impact: Safer operations and easier onboarding for roadmap automation

decision: Use default RESPONSIBLE_ID=1 for roadmap publication
reason: Portal rejects task creation without assignee and explicit responsible IDs were not provided per-task
impact: Enabled successful creation of all roadmap tasks in Bitrix24 project

decision: Persist Bitrix task mapping in repository context
reason: Need deterministic key->task ID map for subsequent automated status updates
impact: Supports continuous repo-to-Bitrix execution tracking

decision: Duplicate epic identifiers in Bitrix task tags
reason: Need fast filtering/grouping by epic in board views
impact: Improved operational visibility without changing task graph

decision: Execute only RD-001 and pause subsequent tasks
reason: User requested to review outcomes after first stage before continuing
impact: Controlled rollout and early feedback loop

decision: Make VPS deploy synchronization check mandatory after every push
reason: Autodeploy can silently stop when VPS working tree becomes dirty or diverges from origin
impact: Prevents drift between repository state and production runtime

decision: Drive Bitrix24 kanban movement from live stage metadata fetched via webhook
reason: Portal may use custom stage IDs and empty SYSTEM_TYPE for non-default columns
impact: Status transitions now reliably move cards visually in the real kanban board

decision: Enforce task naming and description conventions in automation
reason: Need consistent human-readable titles and Russian descriptions for project operations
impact: Improved board readability and standardized execution artifacts

decision: Define RD-002 baseline quality checks as script-first with CI reuse
reason: Need deterministic minimal checks runnable locally and in GitHub Actions
impact: Reduced drift between local validation and CI execution

decision: Allow frontend lint skip when pnpm is absent locally
reason: Developer environment may not always have Node toolchain while CI has deterministic setup
impact: Local checks remain usable while CI enforces full baseline

decision: Introduce dedicated RD-105 for V1 installation success milestone
reason: Existing roadmap did not explicitly track the requested installer+settings deliverable
impact: Made V1 scope explicit and traceable in both repository and Bitrix24

decision: Add RD-106 for V2 creation after V1 approval
reason: User requested explicit version transition workflow after V1 completion
impact: Versioning step is now planned, visible, and dependency-bound in roadmap

decision: Adopt epic-root task model in Bitrix24 with roadmap tasks as subtasks
reason: Need visually clear board structure for users with explicit epic ownership
impact: Improves planning readability and navigation in task board

decision: Keep gantt dependencies and parent hierarchy simultaneously with conflict fallback
reason: Bitrix24 rejects parent links that introduce dependency cycles
impact: Automation remains robust while preserving maximal hierarchy coverage

decision: Add hierarchical numbering to Bitrix24 subtask titles
reason: User requested more readable visual structure like 2/2.1/2.2 on board
impact: Improved operability and visual traceability in kanban and task lists

decision: Standardize completion workflow with Bitrix task result + epic auto-close sync commands
reason: Need mandatory, repeatable process for visual control in Bitrix24 while keeping repository as source of truth
impact: Completed tasks now include commit-linked result records; epic closure state is synchronized and human-readable

decision: Use legacy task.comment.add with form-encoded payload before tasks.task.result.addFromComment
reason: task.commentitem.add comment IDs are rejected by task result method on target portal
impact: Ensures completion result workflow is executable in current Bitrix24 environment

decision: Take RD-101 into work as next roadmap task after closing cycle with push and workflow artifact updates
reason: User requested sequential execution of step 1 (push/verify-sync) and step 2 (update task in all systems); RD-101 is first unblocked Stage 1 task
impact: Repository status and plan reflect RD-101 IN_PROGRESS; Bitrix24 sync pending where B24_WEBHOOK_URL is available

decision: Make VPS sync verification service list configurable
reason: verify-sync failed on servers without optional b24-webhook systemd unit
impact: Default check now validates b24-ai-starter only; extra units can be required via VPS_REQUIRED_SERVICES

decision: Use mirror.gcr.io for Python base image in backend Dockerfile
reason: VPS startup failed due to Docker Hub anonymous pull rate limits
impact: Improved reliability of first boot and redeploy on clean hosts

decision: Remove forced Connection upgrade header from cloud-init nginx template
reason: Nuxt dev server proxy emitted invalid connection header and caused HTTP 500
impact: Public root route became stable through nginx reverse proxy

decision: Bootstrap new VPS with dedicated deploy-webhook service
reason: Need deterministic auto-deploy flow on freshly recreated server
impact: Server now supports signed GitHub webhook deploys and post-push sync checks

decision: Run webhook receiver as deploy user with restricted local proxy exposure
reason: Avoid root git safe-directory issues and reduce privilege surface
impact: Signed push events execute b24-deploy successfully without ownership conflicts

decision: Use hard reset + clean in b24-deploy before restart
reason: Auto-deploy must not fail because of accidental local drift on VPS
impact: Deploy command is idempotent and reproducible for webhook/manual triggers

decision: Enable frontend demo fallback mode when B24 frame/JWT is unavailable
reason: User observed white screens and could not validate pages after install
impact: Pages now render deterministic test data and remain observable outside full B24 runtime context

decision: Confirm installation persistence by direct DB verification on VPS
reason: Need explicit proof that install flow writes account/tokens to backend storage
impact: Installation success can be audited operationally via bitrix24account table state

decision: Make roadmap sync idempotent via embedded Bitrix IDs and project discovery
reason: Auto sync-epic-structure kept recreating duplicate epic roots when mapping drifted
impact: Epic/task creation is now guarded by ROADMAP_TASKS bitrix_ids + live project lookup, preventing duplicate entities on repeated runs

decision: Make context-engineering baseline skills mandatory for all tasks
reason: User requested deterministic activation of context-engineering discipline in every session
impact: Project rules/workflows now require context-engineering-collection + context-fundamentals + context-optimization before task-specific skills

decision: Switch roadmap numbering to epic-aware hierarchical format
reason: Need stable naming convention N -> N.1 -> N.1.1 across epics/tasks/subtasks
impact: Bitrix24 titles now encode epic ownership and nesting depth directly in task names

decision: Adopt supervisor + epic-agent operating model with filesystem handoff contract
reason: Need one dedicated agent per epic and guaranteed propagation of epic outcomes into shared project memory
impact: Epic execution is now context-isolated per folder under .agent/context/epics with mandatory supervisor merge into global context artifacts

decision: Enforce dependency gate and blocked-state workflow for cross-epic prerequisites
reason: Need sequential and professional execution where dependent work cannot start before prerequisite completion
impact: Agents now must pause blocked tasks, wait for dependency completion, refresh context, and only then continue implementation

decision: Make chat start template operational for supervisor and epic-agent launch
reason: Need deterministic chat bootstrap that immediately tells agents how to start orchestration and per-epic execution
impact: Reduced startup ambiguity; supervisor and epic-agent runs now start from ready-to-use message templates with dependency gate steps

decision: Assign EPIC-FND->RD-004 and EPIC-INS->RD-101 after dependency-gate validation
reason: Need to start first supervised epic cycle for epics 1 and 2 without violating cross-task prerequisites
impact: Both epic-agents have clear next tasks and DoD/test focus; blocked downstream tasks in EPIC-INS remain gated until RD-101 completion

decision: Version installer contract and enforce idempotent install upsert for RD-101
reason: RD-102/RD-103/RD-104 require stable installer interface and deterministic replay-safe behavior before UI/scope/sync work
impact: Installer now exposes contract endpoint, stores fingerprint metadata, and keeps mapping container versioned for downstream tasks

decision: Close RD-101 and move RD-102/RD-103 to IN_PROGRESS
reason: Dependency gate for next installer tasks is satisfied only after RD-101 completion
impact: EPIC-INS moved from contract baseline to implementation of mapping UI and scope diagnostics

decision: Implement installer mapping and scope checks through dedicated APIs and settings-page UI
reason: Need end-to-end flow to continue EPIC-INS without waiting for full UX polish
impact: RD-102/RD-103 now have functional backend endpoints and visible frontend integration

decision: Prioritize error-free landing and direct configuration CTA
reason: User requested no startup errors and explicit path to RD-102 from main page
impact: Main page now routes directly to settings; backend health alert no longer blocks first interaction

decision: Use resilient settings loading with graceful fallbacks
reason: Settings page could hang when backend was unreachable despite demo mode expectations
impact: RD-102 screen now opens predictably in both connected and degraded environments

decision: Replace raw JSON mapping editor with structured form UI in settings
reason: Need a practical, operator-friendly RD-102 interface for daily use
impact: Users can configure mapping through explicit fields and validation without manual JSON crafting

decision: Pivot RD-102 to guided CRM entity creation flow
reason: Need deterministic setup and remove manual smart-process selection to reduce operator mistakes
impact: Installer now guides users through crm.automatedsolution.add and crm.type.add for Goals with progress and direct links

decision: Close RD-004 and EPIC-FND immediately after production UAT confirmation
reason: User confirmed Django admin flow works and requested full closure with history + commit
impact: RD-004 moved to DONE, EPIC-FND marked completed in Bitrix24, closure artifacts synchronized
